title: National Consumer Price Index Increases by 0.8% Year-on-Year in December 2025
description: In December 2025, the national consumer price index (CPI) rose by 0.8% year-on-year, driven by increases in food and non-food prices. Urban areas saw a 0.9% increase, while rural areas experienced a 0.6% rise. Monthly, the CPI increased by 0.2%. Overall, the CPI remained unchanged for the year.

key_points.details: In December 2025, the national consumer price index (CPI) rose by 0.8% year-on-year, driven by food price increases, particularly fresh vegetables.
Urban areas experienced a higher CPI increase of 0.9% compared to 0.6% in rural areas, indicating a disparity in price changes across regions.
Food prices rose by 1.1% year-on-year, with fresh vegetable prices surging by 18.2%, significantly impacting the overall CPI increase.

full_content.analysis: The December 2025 CPI data reveals significant price changes, particularly in food categories, with urban areas experiencing a greater increase than rural ones. The notable rise in fresh vegetable prices by 18.2% has a substantial impact on the overall CPI, highlighting the volatility in food prices. This situation presents both challenges and opportunities for food retailers and wholesalers, as they may see increased demand and revenue. Conversely, consumer services may face pressure to adjust pricing in response to rising costs, potentially affecting consumer behavior and spending patterns in the broader economy.

chinese_original:
2025年12月份，全国居民消费价格同比上涨0.8%。其中，城市上涨0.9%，农村上涨0.6%；食品价格上涨1.1%，非食品价格上涨0.8%；消费品价格上涨1.0%，服务价格上涨0.6%。
12月份，全国居民消费价格环比上涨0.2%。其中，城市上涨0.2%，农村上涨0.2%；食品价格上涨0.3%，非食品价格上涨0.1%；消费品价格上涨0.3%，服务价格持平。
2025年全年，全国居民消费价格与上年持平。
一、各类商品及服务价格同比变动情况
12月份，食品烟酒类价格同比上涨0.8%，影响CPI（居民消费价格指数）上涨约0.24个百分点。食品中，鲜菜价格上涨18.2%，影响CPI上涨约0.39个百分点；鲜果价格上涨4.4%，影响CPI上涨约0.09个百分点；水产品价格上涨1.6%，影响CPI上涨约0.03个百分点；蛋类价格下降12.7%，影响CPI下降约0.09个百分点；畜肉类价格下降6.1%，影响CPI下降约0.19个百分点，其中猪肉价格下降14.6%，影响CPI下降约0.20个百分点；粮食价格下降0.3%，影响CPI下降约0.01个百分点。
其他七大类价格同比五涨两降。其中，其他用品及服务、生活用品及服务、医疗保健价格分别上涨17.4%、2.2%和1.8%，衣着、教育文化娱乐价格分别上涨1.7%和0.9%；交通通信、居住价格分别下降2.6%和0.2%。
二、各类商品及服务价格环比变动情况
12月份，食品烟酒类价格环比上涨0.2%，影响CPI上涨约0.05个百分点。食品中，鲜果价格上涨2.6%，影响CPI上涨约0.05个百分点；鲜菜价格上涨0.8%，影响CPI上涨约0.02个百分点；畜肉类价格下降0.8%，影响CPI下降约0.02个百分点，其中猪肉价格下降1.7%，影响CPI下降约0.02个百分点。
其他七大类价格环比四涨两平一降。其中，其他用品及服务、生活用品及服务价格分别上涨2.8%和0.4%，教育文化娱乐、医疗保健价格均上涨0.1%；衣着、交通通信价格均持平；居住价格下降0.1%。
2025年12月份居民消费价格主要数据
点击下载：相关数据表
附注
1.指标解释
居民消费价格指数（Consumer Price Index，简称CPI）是度量居民生活消费品和服务价格水平随着时间变动的相对数，综合反映居民购买的生活消费品和服务价格水平的变动情况。
2.统计范围
居民消费价格统计调查涵盖全国城乡居民生活消费的食品烟酒、衣着、居住、生活用品及服务、交通通信、教育文化娱乐、医疗保健、其他用品及服务等8大类、268个基本分类的商品与服务价格。
3.调查方法
采用抽样调查方法抽选确定调查网点，按照“定人、定点、定时”的原则，直接派人到调查网点或从互联网采集原始价格。数据来源于全国31个省（区、市）约500个市县、10万余家价格调查点，包括商场（店）、超市、农贸（生鲜）市场、服务网点和互联网电商等。
4.数据说明
由于“四舍五入”原因，有时会出现合计数据与分类数据高值或低值相同的情况。
english_translation:
In December 2025, the national consumer price index (CPI) for residents increased by 0.8% year-on-year. Among them, urban areas saw an increase of 0.9%, while rural areas experienced a rise of 0.6%; food prices rose by 1.1%, and non-food prices increased by 0.8%; consumer goods prices went up by 1.0%, and service prices rose by 0.6%. 

In December, the national consumer price index increased by 0.2% month-on-month. Urban areas saw a rise of 0.2%, and rural areas also increased by 0.2%; food prices rose by 0.3%, while non-food prices increased by 0.1%; consumer goods prices went up by 0.3%, and service prices remained unchanged. 

For the entire year of 2025, the national consumer price index remained unchanged compared to the previous year. 

I. Year-on-year price changes for various goods and services 
In December, the prices of food, tobacco, and alcohol increased by 0.8% year-on-year, contributing approximately 0.24 percentage points to the CPI increase. Within food, fresh vegetable prices rose by 18.2%, contributing approximately 0.39 percentage points to the CPI increase; fresh fruit prices increased by 4.4%, contributing approximately 0.09 percentage points; aquatic product prices rose by 1.6%, contributing approximately 0.03 percentage points; egg prices decreased by 12.7%, contributing approximately 0.09 percentage points to the CPI decrease; meat prices fell by 6.1%, contributing approximately 0.19 percentage points to the CPI decrease, with pork prices decreasing by 14.6%, contributing approximately 0.20 percentage points; grain prices decreased by 0.3%, contributing approximately 0.01 percentage points to the CPI decrease. 

Among the other seven major categories, five saw price increases while two experienced declines. Specifically, prices for other goods and services, daily necessities, and healthcare rose by 17.4%, 2.2%, and 1.8%, respectively; clothing and education, culture, and entertainment prices increased by 1.7% and 0.9%, respectively; transportation and communication, and housing prices decreased by 2.6% and 0.2%, respectively. 

II. Month-on-month price changes for various goods and services 
In December, the prices of food, tobacco, and alcohol increased by 0.2% month-on-month, contributing approximately 0.05 percentage points to the CPI increase. Within food, fresh fruit prices rose by 2.6%, contributing approximately 0.05 percentage points; fresh vegetable prices increased by 0.8%, contributing approximately 0.02 percentage points; meat prices decreased by 0.8%, contributing approximately 0.02 percentage points, with pork prices decreasing by 1.7%, contributing approximately 0.02 percentage points. 

Among the other seven major categories, four saw price increases, two remained unchanged, and one experienced a decline. Specifically, prices for other goods and services, and daily necessities rose by 2.8% and 0.4%, respectively; education, culture, and entertainment, and healthcare prices both increased by 0.1%; clothing and transportation and communication prices remained unchanged; housing prices decreased by 0.1%. 

Main data on resident consumer prices for December 2025 
Click to download: Related data table 

Notes 
1. Indicator explanation 
The Consumer Price Index (CPI) measures the relative change in the price level of consumer goods and services over time, reflecting the changes in the price level of goods and services purchased by residents. 

2. Statistical scope 
The consumer price statistical survey covers eight major categories of goods and services consumed by urban and rural residents nationwide, including food, tobacco, and alcohol; clothing; housing; daily necessities and services; transportation and communication; education, culture, and entertainment; healthcare; and other goods and services, totaling 268 basic classifications. 

3. Survey method 
A sampling survey method is used to select survey points, adhering to the principle of "designated personnel, designated locations, designated times," with personnel dispatched directly to survey points or original prices collected from the internet. Data is sourced from approximately 500 cities and counties across 31 provinces (regions, municipalities) and over 100,000 price survey points, including shopping malls, supermarkets, agricultural markets, service points, and e-commerce platforms. 

4. Data explanation 
Due to rounding, there may be instances where the total data matches the high or low values of classified data.

impact_analysis.details: The rise in food prices, especially fresh vegetables, directly benefits food retailers and wholesalers, potentially increasing their revenue.
The overall increase in the CPI may lead to higher costs for consumer services, affecting pricing strategies and consumer spending.

title: Heilongjiang Securities Regulatory Bureau issues warning to Jianghai Securities Co., Ltd.
description: Jianghai Securities Co., Ltd. was found to have third-party involvement in investor solicitation and inadequate personnel management, violating multiple regulations. The Heilongjiang Securities Regulatory Bureau issued a warning letter and mandated rectification within 30 days.

key_points.details: Jianghai Securities Co., Ltd. faces regulatory scrutiny due to third-party involvement in investor solicitation and inadequate personnel management.
The company is required to rectify compliance issues and report back within 30 days, highlighting the importance of adherence to securities regulations.

full_content.analysis: The warning letter issued to Jianghai Securities Co., Ltd. highlights significant compliance failures, including improper investor solicitation and inadequate personnel management. These violations not only breach multiple regulatory articles but also pose risks to the integrity of the financial services sector. The Heilongjiang Securities Regulatory Bureau's directive for rectification within 30 days emphasizes the necessity for firms in the diversified financial services industry to maintain rigorous compliance standards. This situation serves as a critical reminder for all financial institutions to prioritize adherence to regulations to safeguard investor trust and market stability.

chinese_original:
江海证券有限公司：

经查，你公司存在以下问题：一是第三方介入投资者招揽活动，佣金收取不规范；二是从业人员管理不到位，聘任未取得证券从业资格的人员从事证券业务和相关管理工作，未有效防范从业人员利用未公开信息买卖证券等行为，未按规定及时报送有关人员任职备案材料等。

上述情形违反了《证券经纪业务管理办法》（证监会令第204号）第九条、第二十八条，《证券基金经营机构董事、监事、高级管理人员及从业人员监督管理办法》（证监会令第227号）第三条第三款、第十一条第一款、第三十二条第一款、第三十五条、第四十五条，《证券公司和证券投资基金管理公司合规管理办法》（证监会令第166号）第六条第五项的规定。

根据《证券经纪业务管理办法》第四十三条、《证券基金经营机构董事、监事、高级管理人员及从业人员监督管理办法》第五十一条第一款、《证券公司和证券投资基金管理公司合规管理办法》第三十二条第一款的规定，我局决定对你公司采取出具警示函的行政监管措施。你公司应高度重视上述问题，采取有效措施进行整改，切实提高合规管理水平，并在收到本决定书之日起30日内向我局书面报告整改情况。

如果对本监督管理措施不服，可以在收到本决定书之日起60日内向中国证券监督管理委员会提出行政复议申请，也可以在收到本决定书之日起6个月内向有管辖权的人民法院提起诉讼。复议与诉讼期间，上述监督管理措施不停止执行。

黑龙江证监局

2025年12月26日
english_translation:
Jianghai Securities Co., Ltd.:

Upon investigation, your company has the following issues: First, there is third-party involvement in investor solicitation activities, and the commission collection is not standardized; second, the management of personnel is inadequate, with unqualified individuals engaging in securities business and related management work, failing to effectively prevent personnel from trading securities using non-public information, and not submitting the required personnel appointment filing materials in a timely manner as stipulated.

The above circumstances violate Article 9 and Article 28 of the "Administrative Measures for Securities Brokerage Business" (CSRC Order No. 204), Article 3, Paragraph 3, Article 11, Paragraph 1, Article 32, Paragraph 1, Article 35, and Article 45 of the "Supervision and Administration Measures for Directors, Supervisors, Senior Management Personnel, and Practitioners of Securities and Fund Management Institutions" (CSRC Order No. 227), and Item 5 of Article 6 of the "Compliance Management Measures for Securities Companies and Securities Investment Fund Management Companies" (CSRC Order No. 166).

In accordance with Article 43 of the "Administrative Measures for Securities Brokerage Business," Article 51, Paragraph 1 of the "Supervision and Administration Measures for Directors, Supervisors, Senior Management Personnel, and Practitioners of Securities and Fund Management Institutions," and Article 32, Paragraph 1 of the "Compliance Management Measures for Securities Companies and Securities Investment Fund Management Companies," our bureau has decided to take administrative regulatory measures by issuing a warning letter to your company. Your company should attach great importance to the above issues, take effective measures for rectification, and significantly improve compliance management levels, and report the rectification situation in writing to our bureau within 30 days from the date of receipt of this decision.

If you are dissatisfied with this supervisory measure, you may apply for administrative reconsideration to the China Securities Regulatory Commission within 60 days from the date of receipt of this decision, or you may file a lawsuit in a competent people's court within 6 months from the date of receipt of this decision. The aforementioned supervisory measures shall remain in effect during the reconsideration and litigation period.

Heilongjiang Securities Regulatory Bureau

December 26, 2025

impact_analysis.details: The warning issued to Jianghai Securities may impact banks involved in securities transactions, emphasizing the need for stringent compliance.
This regulatory action underscores the importance of compliance in diversified financial services, potentially affecting investor confidence.

title: China's Communist Party Develops National Security System and Capabilities
description: The Fourth Plenary Session of the 20th Central Committee of the Communist Party of China outlined a strategy to modernize the national security system, emphasizing a holistic and collaborative approach to address complex security challenges and ensure long-term stability.

key_points.details: The policy emphasizes a holistic view of national security, integrating various fields such as politics, economy, and technology to enhance governance.
It advocates for collaborative governance, enhancing interaction among multiple entities to effectively address complex security threats.
The policy promotes dynamic thinking in security governance, establishing a closed-loop mechanism for pre-warning, response, and post-evaluation of security risks.

full_content.analysis: The policy outlines a comprehensive framework for national security governance, emphasizing the need for a holistic, collaborative, and dynamic approach. By integrating various fields such as political, economic, and technological security, it aims to create a unified strategy that addresses the complexities of modern security threats. The focus on collaborative governance highlights the importance of multi-entity interactions, ensuring that different sectors work together effectively. Additionally, the dynamic risk management approach aims to establish a responsive governance mechanism that can adapt to evolving security challenges, thereby enhancing national stability and resilience.

chinese_original:
国家安全是民族复兴的根基，是安邦定国的重要基石。党的二十届四中全会围绕“推进国家安全体系和能力现代化，建设更高水平平安中国”进行系统部署，强调“巩固集中统一、高效权威的国家安全领导体制，加快构建新安全格局，增强维护和塑造国家安全战略主动”。当前，世界百年未有之大变局加速演进，传统安全问题与非传统安全问题交织叠加，国家安全的内涵比历史上任何时候都要丰富，国家安全的时空领域比历史上任何时候都要宽广，影响国家安全的内外因素比历史上任何时候都要复杂。构建新安全格局，要牢固树立总体国家安全观，运用马克思主义的系统思维方法，通盘考虑基本国情、发展阶段、综合国力以及大国博弈等多个维度，提出整体性前瞻性应对方略。

一

习近平总书记指出：“各级党委和政府要坚持系统思维，进一步树立发展是硬道理、安全也是硬道理的理念，在工作中自觉把发展和安全统一起来，共同谋划、一体部署、相互促进。要坚持全国一盘棋、上下齐发力，通过抓好一地一域一业的安全为国家整体安全创造条件，通过及时有效解决一个个安全问题为国家长治久安筑牢根基。”国家安全的不同领域相互联系、不可分割，维护国家安全需要统筹兼顾。系统思维是运用系统观点，全面地、普遍联系地观察事物，妥善处理各种重大关系的思维方法，强调整体性、协同性和动态性。

运用整体性思维构建“大安全”体系。当今时代，安全问题呈现出高度复杂性和关联性，传统“碎片化”的安全治理模式已难以满足现实需求。运用整体性思维构建涵盖多领域多层级的“大安全”体系成为必然选择。整体性思维主张从事物的整体、内在联系和动态发展中把握事物发展的本质和规律，坚持整体优先、聚焦关联互动、突出层次结构。国家安全涉及政治、经济、科技、生物等多个领域，其中，政治安全是国家安全的根本，稳定的政治环境为经济发展、科技创新等提供坚强保障；经济安全是国家安全的基础，强大的经济实力为其他领域安全提供物质支撑；科技安全直接关系国家核心竞争力；生物安全则与人民生命健康息息相关。只有将诸多领域视为一个有机整体，打破领域分割和部门壁垒，形成协同联动的治理格局，才能有效防范和应对各类安全威胁，实现国家长治久安和人民安居乐业。

运用协同性思维强化多主体联动。协同性思维强调通过有意识的设计、管理和优化系统内部及各相关系统之间的互动关系和合作过程，使各种要素、资源、行动在时间、空间和功能上相互配合、优势互补，从而实现整体效能的最大化。实践中，各职能部门在安全事务中扮演着独特角色，通过强化多主体间联动机制，建立常态化的沟通机制、联合应急处置预案，能够大幅提升应对复杂安全威胁的能力。例如，不同地区面临的安全风险各具地域特征，但又相互影响。在应对跨境犯罪问题上，边境地区加强出入境管控，内陆地区深挖犯罪网络，能够更好实现对跨境犯罪的全链条打击。各地通过区域安全合作会议、联合执法行动等方式，有助于打破地域限制，形成防控合力。再如，军地联动在国家安全体系中居于重要位置。军队拥有强大的作战能力和专业技术，地方则具备丰富的社会资源和广泛的信息渠道。在抢险救灾中，军队迅速投入兵力进行救援，地方负责组织物资保障、安置受灾群众，只有实现军地高效协同，才能形成强大合力。

运用动态性思维完善安全治理闭环机制。动态性思维强调从事物的运动、变化、演化和发展过程中去理解和把握事物的本质。安全风险具有传导性和耦合性等特点，处在不断的发展变化过程中，这要求我们运用动态性思维，建立“事前预警—事中处置—事后评估”的安全治理闭环机制。事前预警是安全治理的第一道防线，通过运用传感器、大数据、人工智能等先进技术手段，强化市民热线、综治中心等平台功能，能够对政治、经济、社会等多领域数据进行实时监测与分析，及时捕捉和回应潜在安全风险。一旦风险发生，事中处置必须迅速、有效，而这需要科学有效的应急预案、各部门之间的紧密配合，以形成高效的应急响应机制。事后评估是安全治理闭环机制的重要环节，对安全事件进行全面、科学的评估，分析事件发生的原因及耦合因素、处置过程中的优点与不足，有助于发现安全防护体系存在的漏洞，及时进行修复和升级，并完善相关制度和应急预案。

二

新征程上，只有适应复杂多变的安全形势，将系统思维转化为可执行、可评估的全要素协同、全链条治理、全手段运用的行动框架，加快构建新安全格局，才能实现国家安全治理的科学化、精准化与高效化。

整合安全资源，实现全要素协同。全要素协同旨在打破安全资源分散、部门分割的“碎片化”困境和“孤岛效应”，通过建立高效的协同机制实现资源优化配置。为此，应完善“四梁八柱”制度体系，以国家安全法为统领，健全法治体系、战略体系、政策体系；建立风险监测预警、应急管理、危机管控、事后恢复等专业化机制；推动安全治理重心下移，街道社区、企事业单位、社会组织和居民群众等主体积极参与，落实安全责任，形成全社会共同维护国家安全的强大合力。同时，完善京津冀、长三角等区域联防联控机制，各区域通过建立信息共享平台、联合演练等方式，实现区域内安全力量的协同联动，共同应对自然灾害、公共卫生事件等跨区域安全威胁。注重跨领域协同，完善共建“一带一路”安全保障体系，将安全与发展有机结合，在推动共建国家经济合作的同时，通过构建风险预警、应急处置等机制，统筹应对政治、经济、文化等多领域安全挑战。

强化过程管控，实现全链条治理。全链条治理着眼安全风险防控全过程，旨在形成从源头预防到末端处置的闭环管理，以有效防范风险传导和次生灾害。源头治理强调通过科学的风险评估机制，提前识别潜在安全隐患，将隐患扼杀在萌芽。过程管控依托大数据、人工智能等先进技术，构建智能化预警体系。这需要整合多部门数据资源，运用算法大模型对各类安全风险进行动态分析与预警，实现风险的早发现、早研判、早预防。末端处置注重应急预案体系的完善与实战化演练。例如，针对自然灾害、事故灾难等突发事件，制定详细的应急预案，并通过定期演练检验预案的可行性，提升应急响应能力与处置效率。

创新治理工具，实现全手段运用。全手段运用旨在综合运用多种手段，形成国家安全治理合力。例如，聚焦意识形态安全，通过加强主流价值观传播、提升舆论引导能力，抵御外部势力的意识形态渗透，筑牢思想防线。以产业链“链长制”为抓手，通过建立产业链供应链安全保障体系，强化关键环节、核心技术的自主可控，保障国家经济安全。推动量子通信、区块链等前沿技术创新应用，提升网络安全防护能力，应对数字时代的新型安全威胁。完善国家安全领域法律法规体系，为国家安全治理提供坚实法律依据，提升国家安全体系和能力现代化水平。

（作者：袁振龙，系北京市习近平新时代中国特色社会主义思想研究中心特约研究员、北京市社会科学院综合治理研究所所长）
english_translation:
National security is the foundation of national rejuvenation and an important cornerstone for maintaining stability and governance. The Fourth Plenary Session of the 20th Central Committee of the Communist Party of China systematically deployed efforts around "promoting the modernization of the national security system and capabilities, and building a higher level of a peaceful China," emphasizing the need to "consolidate a centralized, unified, efficient, and authoritative national security leadership system, accelerate the construction of a new security pattern, and enhance the initiative in maintaining and shaping national security strategies." Currently, the world is undergoing unprecedented changes not seen in a century, with traditional and non-traditional security issues intertwining and overlapping. The connotation of national security is richer than ever in history, the spatial and temporal dimensions of national security are broader than ever, and the internal and external factors affecting national security are more complex than ever. To construct a new security pattern, it is essential to firmly establish a holistic view of national security, employing the systematic thinking methods of Marxism to comprehensively consider multiple dimensions such as basic national conditions, development stages, comprehensive national strength, and great power competition, and propose an overall and forward-looking response strategy.

I

General Secretary Xi Jinping pointed out: "Party committees and governments at all levels must adhere to systematic thinking, further establish the concept that development is a hard truth and security is also a hard truth, and consciously unify development and security in their work, jointly plan, deploy in an integrated manner, and promote each other. We must adhere to a nationwide approach, with efforts from top to bottom, creating conditions for overall national security by ensuring the safety of specific regions, sectors, and industries, and laying a solid foundation for long-term national stability by timely and effectively addressing individual security issues." Different fields of national security are interconnected and inseparable, and maintaining national security requires a comprehensive approach. Systematic thinking is a method of thinking that uses a systemic perspective to observe things comprehensively and in a universally connected manner, properly handling various major relationships, emphasizing integrity, coordination, and dynamism.

Using holistic thinking to construct a "big security" system. In today's era, security issues exhibit high complexity and interconnectivity, making the traditional "fragmented" security governance model inadequate to meet real needs. It has become an inevitable choice to use holistic thinking to build a "big security" system that encompasses multiple fields and levels. Holistic thinking advocates grasping the essence and laws of development from the overall, internal connections, and dynamic developments of things, prioritizing the whole, focusing on interrelated interactions, and highlighting hierarchical structures. National security involves multiple fields such as politics, economy, technology, and biology, among which political security is fundamental to national security, providing a strong guarantee for economic development and technological innovation; economic security is the foundation of national security, with strong economic strength providing material support for security in other fields; technological security directly relates to national core competitiveness; and biological security is closely related to the health and lives of the people. Only by viewing these various fields as an organic whole, breaking down barriers between fields and departments, and forming a collaborative governance pattern can we effectively prevent and respond to various security threats, achieving long-term national stability and the well-being of the people.

Using collaborative thinking to strengthen multi-entity interaction. Collaborative thinking emphasizes consciously designing, managing, and optimizing the interactive relationships and cooperation processes within the system and among relevant systems, allowing various elements, resources, and actions to coordinate and complement each other in time, space, and function, thereby maximizing overall effectiveness. In practice, various functional departments play unique roles in security affairs. By strengthening the interaction mechanism among multiple entities and establishing regular communication mechanisms and joint emergency response plans, the ability to respond to complex security threats can be significantly enhanced. For example, different regions face security risks with distinct regional characteristics, yet they influence each other. In addressing cross-border crime issues, border areas strengthen entry and exit controls, while inland areas delve into criminal networks, enabling a more comprehensive crackdown on cross-border crime. Regional security cooperation meetings and joint law enforcement actions help break down geographical limitations and form a collaborative force for prevention and control. Furthermore, military-civilian interaction occupies an important position in the national security system. The military possesses strong combat capabilities and specialized technologies, while local areas have abundant social resources and extensive information channels. In disaster response, the military quickly deploys forces for rescue, while local authorities organize material support and resettle affected populations. Only by achieving efficient collaboration between military and civilian sectors can a powerful synergy be formed.

Using dynamic thinking to improve the security governance closed-loop mechanism. Dynamic thinking emphasizes understanding and grasping the essence of things through their movement, change, evolution, and development processes. Security risks have characteristics such as transmission and coupling, and are in a constant state of development and change. This requires us to use dynamic thinking to establish a closed-loop mechanism for security governance that includes "pre-warning—response—post-evaluation." Pre-warning is the first line of defense in security governance. By utilizing advanced technologies such as sensors, big data, and artificial intelligence, and enhancing the functions of citizen hotlines and comprehensive governance centers, real-time monitoring and analysis of data across political, economic, and social fields can be conducted to timely capture and respond to potential security risks. Once risks occur, the response must be swift and effective, which requires scientifically effective emergency plans and close cooperation among departments to form an efficient emergency response mechanism. Post-evaluation is an important link in the closed-loop mechanism of security governance, involving comprehensive and scientific assessments of security incidents, analyzing the causes and coupling factors of incidents, as well as the strengths and weaknesses in the response process, which helps identify vulnerabilities in the security protection system, timely repair and upgrade them, and improve relevant systems and emergency plans.

II

On the new journey, only by adapting to the complex and changing security situation and transforming systematic thinking into an executable and assessable action framework that encompasses all elements, all-chain governance, and all means of application, can we accelerate the construction of a new security pattern and achieve scientific, precise, and efficient national security governance.

Integrating security resources to achieve all-element collaboration. All-element collaboration aims to break the "fragmented" dilemma of dispersed security resources and departmental segmentation, and to achieve optimized resource allocation through the establishment of efficient collaborative mechanisms. To this end, it is necessary to improve the "four beams and eight pillars" institutional system, with the National Security Law as the overarching framework, to enhance the legal system, strategic system, and policy system; establish specialized mechanisms for risk monitoring and early warning, emergency management, crisis control, and post-event recovery; promote the decentralization of security governance, encouraging active participation from communities, enterprises, social organizations, and residents to fulfill security responsibilities and form a strong collective effort to safeguard national security. At the same time, improve the regional joint prevention and control mechanisms in areas such as Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei and the Yangtze River Delta, where regions can achieve collaborative interaction of security forces through the establishment of information-sharing platforms and joint drills to jointly address cross-regional security threats such as natural disasters and public health incidents. Emphasizing cross-field collaboration, improve the security guarantee system for the Belt and Road Initiative, organically combining security and development, while promoting economic cooperation among countries, and establishing mechanisms for risk early warning and emergency response to comprehensively address security challenges across political, economic, and cultural fields.

Strengthening process control to achieve all-chain governance. All-chain governance focuses on the entire process of security risk prevention and control, aiming to form a closed-loop management system from source prevention to end response, effectively preventing risk transmission and secondary disasters. Source governance emphasizes identifying potential security hazards in advance through a scientific risk assessment mechanism, nipping hazards in the bud. Process control relies on advanced technologies such as big data and artificial intelligence to build an intelligent early warning system. This requires integrating data resources from multiple departments and using algorithmic models to dynamically analyze and warn about various security risks, achieving early detection, early assessment, and early prevention of risks. End response emphasizes improving the emergency plan system and conducting practical drills. For example, for sudden events such as natural disasters and accidents, detailed emergency plans should be formulated and regularly drilled to test the feasibility of the plans, enhancing emergency response capabilities and disposal efficiency.

Innovating governance tools to achieve all means of application. All means of application aim to comprehensively utilize various methods to form a collective effort in national security governance. For instance, focusing on ideological security, strengthening the dissemination of mainstream values and enhancing public opinion guidance capabilities to resist ideological infiltration by external forces and fortify the ideological defense line. By leveraging the "chain leader system" for industrial chains, establishing a security guarantee system for supply chains, and strengthening the autonomy and controllability of key links and core technologies to safeguard national economic security. Promoting the innovative application of cutting-edge technologies such as quantum communication and blockchain to enhance cybersecurity capabilities and address new security threats in the digital age. Improving the legal and regulatory framework in the field of national security to provide a solid legal basis for national security governance and enhance the modernization level of the national security system and capabilities.

(Author: Yuan Zhenlong, Special Researcher at the Beijing Xi Jinping Thought on Socialism with Chinese Characteristics for a New Era Research Center, and Director of the Comprehensive Governance Research Institute of the Beijing Academy of Social Sciences)

title: Heilongjiang Securities Regulatory Bureau issues warning to Jianghai Securities Co., Ltd.
description: The Heilongjiang Securities Regulatory Bureau found Jianghai Securities' Harbin Hongjun Street department non-compliant with follow-up visit protocols and documentation. A warning letter was issued, requiring compliance improvements and a rectification report within 30 days.

key_points.details: Jianghai Securities' Harbin branch failed to implement proper follow-up visit systems, violating compliance regulations.
The Heilongjiang Securities Regulatory Bureau issued a warning letter, mandating improved compliance and internal controls.

full_content.analysis: The warning issued to Jianghai Securities' Harbin branch underscores significant compliance failures in the financial services sector. By not adhering to the follow-up visit system, the firm has violated established regulations, prompting the Heilongjiang Securities Regulatory Bureau to take corrective action. This regulatory measure not only serves as a reprimand but also as a call to action for improved internal controls and compliance management across the industry. The implications are clear: firms must prioritize adherence to regulatory frameworks to avoid similar penalties and ensure operational integrity.

chinese_original:
江海证券有限公司哈尔滨红军街证券营业部：

经查，你营业部存在回访制度执行不到位、对个别客户回访不充分、留痕不完整的问题，违反了《证券公司和证券投资基金管理公司合规管理办法》（证监会令第166号）第三条、《关于加强证券经纪业务管理的规定》（证监会公告〔2010〕11号）第三条第四项的规定。

根据《证券公司和证券投资基金管理公司合规管理办法》第三十二条第一款、《关于加强证券经纪业务管理的规定》第八条的规定，我局决定对你营业部采取出具警示函的行政监管措施。你营业部应加强合规内控管理，并在收到本决定书之日起30日内向我局书面报告整改情况。

如果对本监督管理措施不服，可以在收到本决定书之日起60日内向中国证券监督管理委员会提出行政复议申请，也可以在收到本决定书之日起6个月内向有管辖权的人民法院提起诉讼。复议与诉讼期间，上述监督管理措施不停止执行。

黑龙江证监局

2025年12月26日
english_translation:
Jianghai Securities Co., Ltd. Harbin Hongjun Street Securities Business Department:

Upon investigation, it has been found that your business department has issues with the implementation of the follow-up visit system, insufficient follow-up visits for certain clients, and incomplete documentation, which violates Article 3 of the "Administrative Measures for Compliance Management of Securities Companies and Securities Investment Fund Management Companies" (CSRC Order No. 166) and Item 4 of Article 3 of the "Regulations on Strengthening the Management of Securities Brokerage Business" (CSRC Announcement [2010] No. 11).

In accordance with the provisions of the first paragraph of Article 32 of the "Administrative Measures for Compliance Management of Securities Companies and Securities Investment Fund Management Companies" and Article 8 of the "Regulations on Strengthening the Management of Securities Brokerage Business," our bureau has decided to impose an administrative regulatory measure of issuing a warning letter to your business department. Your business department should strengthen compliance and internal control management and submit a written report on rectification to our bureau within 30 days from the date of receipt of this decision.

If you disagree with this supervisory measure, you may apply for administrative reconsideration to the China Securities Regulatory Commission within 60 days from the date of receipt of this decision, or you may file a lawsuit with a competent people's court within 6 months from the date of receipt of this decision. The aforementioned supervisory measures shall remain in effect during the reconsideration and litigation period.

Heilongjiang Securities Regulatory Bureau

December 26, 2025

impact_analysis.details: The warning highlights compliance failures in the banking sector, emphasizing the need for stricter adherence to regulations.
This action signals increased scrutiny on diversified financial services, urging firms to enhance their compliance management.

title: Guizhou Implements Capital Market Reforms to Support Economic Development
description: In 2025, Guizhou enhances its capital market services to support regional economic development, focusing on enterprise listings, direct financing, and innovative financial products. The province aims to improve the quality of services for the real economy and rural revitalization through various initiatives and collaborations.

key_points.details: Guizhou's capital market reforms aim to enhance regional economic development by integrating capital market tools into the provincial industrial system.
The capital market actively supports rural revitalization through financial assistance, education projects, and disaster relief efforts in Guizhou.
New financial products, including technology innovation bonds and green corporate bonds, are being developed to support Guizhou's economic modernization.

full_content.analysis: The policy outlines significant reforms in Guizhou's capital market aimed at fostering regional economic development and modernization. By integrating capital market tools into the provincial industrial system, the initiative seeks to enhance financial services for key sectors, including technology and agriculture. The focus on rural revitalization through financial support and educational initiatives demonstrates a commitment to social responsibility. Furthermore, the introduction of innovative financial products, such as technology innovation bonds, reflects a strategic approach to meet the evolving needs of local enterprises. Overall, these developments position Guizhou's capital market as a pivotal player in driving sustainable economic growth and improving livelihoods in the region.

chinese_original:
2025年，资本市场各方以习近平新时代中国特色社会主义思想为指导，认真贯彻落实党的二十大、二十届历次全会精神，深入学习贯彻习近平总书记在贵州考察时的重要讲话精神，认真落实证监会党委关于深化资本市场改革一系列部署，结合贵州实际，积极探索资本市场服务区域经济发展的“贵州实践”，在服务贵州高质量发展和现代化进程中取得新成效。

一、资本市场服务贵州现代化产业体系建设的体制机制不断完善

省委十三届八次全会审议通过《中共贵州省委关于制定贵州省国民经济和社会发展第十五个五年规划的建议》，对梯次培育推进企业上市、发展和运用多层次资本市场、积极发展股权债券等直接融资作出专门部署，与《贵州省推动资本市场高质量发展实施方案》相互衔接，为“十五五”时期资本市场服务现代化产业体系建设“举旗定向”。全省各方积极推动将资本市场工具运用纳入我省产业体系建设布局，为全方位发挥资本市场功能作用提供了坚实保障。贵州证监局、省委金融办等部门联合编印《贵州省领导干部金融知识读本》，成功举办覆盖全省88个县处级领导干部利用和发展资本市场能力提升专题研讨班，贵州各级领导干部利用和发展资本市场意识能力持续提升。贵阳市创新建立“上市企业之家”，为上市公司提供精准化、全周期的政务服务。贵州资本市场服务中心创新打造资本市场“四库一联盟”服务平台，构建“股债基期”全覆盖、高效链接市场各方的“一站式”服务枢纽，助力加快形成支撑贵州特色现代化产业体系建设的资本市场创新生态。一系列政策机制的不断完善，为助力贵州高质量发展提供了基础和条件。

二、资本市场服务实体经济质效持续增强

贵州资本市场牢牢把握服务实体经济本源，为地方经济社会发展重大战略、重点领域、薄弱环节提供高质量金融产品和服务。2025年，贵州直接融资金额稳步增长，各类市场主体利用资本市场实现直接融资775.46亿元，为我省产业升级、项目建设和民生改善提供了有力支撑。公司信用类债券融资成本持续下降，平均票面利率3.31 %，较上年同期下降49个基点，为企业减负增效提供实实在在支持。证券行业机构持续为我省企业提供高质量股权融资、债券融资、并购重组、财富管理等服务，实现融资485.72亿元。私募基金资产管理规模提升至1940亿元，政府引导基金累计撬动资金超2100亿元，私募股权创投基金投早、投小、投硬科技的规模效应和协同效应已经显现。上市公司质量和投资价值双提升，全省上市公司实施年度分红金额超680亿，回购金额72亿元，均位居西部第一。上市公司获交易所信息披露A类评价家数占比排名全国第五。上市公司常态化走访机制不断完善，协调解决了一批影响企业发展的重点难点问题，走访工作从“全面覆盖”向“精准赋能”转变。

三、资本市场服务“五篇大文章”功能作用取得新突破

贵州资本市场发挥功能作用，全力做好科技金融、绿色金融、普惠金融、养老金融、数字金融“五篇大文章”。2025年，制度框架进一步优化完善，联合多部门出台高质量做好金融“五篇大文章”实施方案，健全覆盖各类企业发展全生命周期需求的“股债基期”产品和服务体系。科技金融在债券市场实现多个创新突破，贵州国资运营公司发行全省首单科技创新公司债，华创证券发行全省首单金融机构科创债，水电九局发行全省首单“知识产权+科技创新”资产证券化产品，中伟股份获批全省首单“一带一路”创新债券，精准支持我省企业科技创新与产业升级。打通县域绿色金融服务壁垒，贵州泽通水资源产业集团获批乡村振兴绿色公司债，成为贵州近年来首个获批新发债券的县级国企，为债券市场更好服务贵州县域经济提供新范例。证券期货行业普惠金融服务效能持续释放，全省首家鸡蛋期货交割库获批，新设1家生猪期货交割库，1家生猪期货交割库顺利开展首次实物交割，2家企业进入交易所产融基地储备库名单。全年累计开展“保险+期货”项目130余个，保障农产品现货金额超15亿元，期货市场服务乡村振兴和企业风险管理的作用进一步发挥。

四、资本市场服务乡村振兴成果日渐丰硕

贵州资本市场始终坚守金融为民初心，积极服务乡村振兴大局，用金融力量书写责任与担当。2025年，资本市场主体接续推动帮扶地区发展和乡村振兴。全国31家证券公司通过“一司一县”机制与省内56个县区签署帮扶协议。贵州证监局联合上交所公益基金会持续深化贵州助学帮教公益项目，新增投入公益基金583万元，实施公益项目6个，累计已覆盖全省160余所中小学，5万名师生受助。“证爱贵州”资本市场教育慈善信托计划持续支持民族地区、革命老区和重点帮扶地区教育事业，向全省180名高考生、680名中考生捐赠奖学金，向贫困地区教师发放稳岗补贴，全年捐赠231.48万元。资本市场主体以责任担当践行扶危济困，“624”“628”洪灾期间向黔东南州捐赠资金物资，帮助受灾地区防汛救灾和灾后重建。证券公司深度参与县域碳金融建设，全省首单林业碳汇CCER项目申报。贵州证监局帮扶点从江县秋里村特色产业、基础设施、人居环境、教育水平不断提质升级，村集体经济收入连续三年突破百万元，为乡村振兴提供了可复制、可推广的实践样本。
english_translation:
In 2025, all parties in the capital market, guided by Xi Jinping's Thought on Socialism with Chinese Characteristics for a New Era, earnestly implement the spirit of the 20th National Congress of the Communist Party of China and the successive plenary sessions of the 20th Central Committee. They deeply study and implement the important speech delivered by General Secretary Xi Jinping during his inspection in Guizhou, and seriously carry out the series of deployments by the Party Committee of the China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC) regarding the deepening of capital market reforms. In conjunction with the actual conditions in Guizhou, they actively explore the "Guizhou practice" of capital market services for regional economic development, achieving new results in supporting Guizhou's high-quality development and modernization process.

I. The institutional mechanisms for capital market services in building Guizhou's modern industrial system are continuously improving.

The 8th Plenary Session of the 13th Provincial Party Committee reviewed and approved the "Suggestions of the Guizhou Provincial Committee of the Communist Party of China on Formulating the 15th Five-Year Plan for National Economic and Social Development of Guizhou Province," which specifically deploys the gradual cultivation and promotion of enterprise listings, the development and utilization of a multi-level capital market, and the active development of equity and bond direct financing. This aligns with the "Implementation Plan for Promoting High-Quality Development of the Capital Market in Guizhou Province," providing direction for the capital market to serve the construction of a modern industrial system during the "15th Five-Year Plan" period. All parties in the province actively promote the integration of capital market tools into the layout of the provincial industrial system, providing a solid guarantee for the comprehensive functioning of the capital market. The Guizhou Securities Regulatory Bureau, the Provincial Financial Office, and other departments jointly compiled the "Financial Knowledge Reader for Guizhou Provincial Leaders," successfully holding specialized seminars to enhance the capacity of leaders from 88 counties and cities in utilizing and developing the capital market, continuously improving the awareness and capability of leaders at all levels in Guizhou regarding capital market utilization and development. Guiyang City has innovatively established the "Home for Listed Companies," providing precise, full-cycle government services for listed companies. The Guizhou Capital Market Service Center has innovatively created the "Four Libraries and One Alliance" service platform, constructing a "one-stop" service hub that covers "stocks, bonds, funds, and futures" and efficiently links all market participants, helping to accelerate the formation of an innovative ecosystem in the capital market that supports the construction of Guizhou's characteristic modern industrial system. The continuous improvement of a series of policy mechanisms provides the foundation and conditions for supporting Guizhou's high-quality development.

II. The quality and effectiveness of capital market services for the real economy continue to enhance.

The Guizhou capital market firmly grasps its original mission of serving the real economy, providing high-quality financial products and services for major strategies, key areas, and weak links in local economic and social development. In 2025, the amount of direct financing in Guizhou steadily increased, with various market entities achieving direct financing of 77.546 billion yuan through the capital market, providing strong support for the province's industrial upgrading, project construction, and improvement of people's livelihoods. The financing costs of corporate credit bonds continued to decline, with an average coupon rate of 3.31%, down 49 basis points from the same period last year, providing tangible support for reducing burdens and increasing efficiency for enterprises. Securities industry institutions continuously provide high-quality services such as equity financing, bond financing, mergers and acquisitions, and wealth management for enterprises in the province, achieving financing of 48.572 billion yuan. The asset management scale of private equity funds has increased to 194 billion yuan, and government-guided funds have leveraged over 210 billion yuan in total. The scale and synergy effects of private equity venture capital funds investing early, small, and in hard technology have begun to show. The quality and investment value of listed companies have both improved, with the total annual dividend amount exceeding 68 billion yuan and share repurchase amount reaching 7.2 billion yuan, both ranking first in the western region. The number of listed companies receiving Class A evaluation for information disclosure from the exchange ranks fifth nationwide. The regular visit mechanism for listed companies continues to improve, coordinating to resolve a number of key issues affecting enterprise development, shifting from "comprehensive coverage" to "precise empowerment."

III. New breakthroughs have been achieved in the functional role of the capital market in serving the "Five Major Articles."

The Guizhou capital market plays its functional role, fully promoting the "Five Major Articles" of financial services: technology finance, green finance, inclusive finance, pension finance, and digital finance. In 2025, the institutional framework was further optimized, with multiple departments jointly issuing high-quality implementation plans for the "Five Major Articles," improving the product and service system covering the full life cycle of various enterprises' development with "stocks, bonds, funds, and futures." Technology finance achieved multiple innovative breakthroughs in the bond market, with the Guizhou State-owned Assets Operation Company issuing the province's first technology innovation corporate bond, Huachuang Securities issuing the province's first financial institution technology innovation bond, and the Water and Electricity Ninth Bureau issuing the province's first "intellectual property + technology innovation" asset securitization product. Zhongwei Co., Ltd. was approved for the province's first "Belt and Road" innovation bond, precisely supporting the technological innovation and industrial upgrading of enterprises in the province. Barriers to green finance services in counties were broken, with Guizhou Zetong Water Resources Industry Group approved for a rural revitalization green corporate bond, becoming the first county-level state-owned enterprise in Guizhou in recent years to be approved for new bond issuance, providing a new model for better serving the county economy through the bond market. The inclusive finance service efficiency of the securities and futures industry continues to be released, with the province's first egg futures delivery warehouse approved, the establishment of one pig futures delivery warehouse, and the successful first physical delivery conducted by one pig futures delivery warehouse, with two enterprises entering the reserve list of the exchange's production and finance base. Over the year, more than 130 "insurance + futures" projects were carried out, ensuring the spot value of agricultural products exceeded 1.5 billion yuan, further enhancing the role of the futures market in serving rural revitalization and enterprise risk management.

IV. The achievements of the capital market in serving rural revitalization are becoming increasingly fruitful.

The Guizhou capital market has always adhered to the original intention of finance for the people, actively serving the overall situation of rural revitalization, and using financial strength to write responsibility and commitment. In 2025, capital market entities continued to promote the development of assisted areas and rural revitalization. Thirty-one securities companies nationwide signed assistance agreements with 56 counties and districts in the province through the "One Company, One County" mechanism. The Guizhou Securities Regulatory Bureau, in conjunction with the Shanghai Stock Exchange Public Welfare Foundation, continues to deepen public welfare projects for education assistance in Guizhou, with an additional investment of 5.83 million yuan in public welfare funds, implementing six public welfare projects that have covered over 160 primary and secondary schools in the province, benefiting 50,000 teachers and students. The "Zheng Ai Guizhou" capital market education charity trust plan continues to support education in ethnic minority areas, revolutionary old areas, and key assistance areas, donating scholarships to 180 college entrance examination candidates and 680 middle school entrance examination candidates, as well as providing stable job subsidies to teachers in impoverished areas, with total donations reaching 2.3148 million yuan for the year. Capital market entities practice their responsibility and commitment to help those in need, donating funds and materials to Qiandongnan Prefecture during the "624" and "628" floods, assisting affected areas in flood prevention, disaster relief, and post-disaster reconstruction. Securities companies are deeply involved in the construction of county-level carbon finance, with the province's first forestry carbon sink CCER project being submitted for approval. The Guizhou Securities Regulatory Bureau's assistance efforts in Qiuli Village, Jiangjun County, have continuously improved the quality of characteristic industries, infrastructure, living environment, and education levels, with collective economic income in the village exceeding one million yuan for three consecutive years, providing a replicable and promotable practical model for rural revitalization.

impact_analysis.details: Banks in Guizhou are expected to benefit from increased financing activities and improved credit conditions due to enhanced capital market services.
Investment services will see growth as the capital market expands, providing new opportunities for equity and bond financing in Guizhou.
Insurance brokers will play a crucial role in facilitating risk management solutions, particularly in agricultural sectors benefiting from futures markets.

title: Jinzhou Bank Co., Ltd. Has Public Offering Securities Investment Fund License Revoked
description: Jinzhou Bank Co., Ltd. applied to cancel its public offering securities investment fund sales business license. The China Securities Regulatory Commission revoked the license in accordance with relevant laws.

key_points.details: Jinzhou Bank Co., Ltd. has had its public offering securities investment fund sales business license revoked by the China Securities Regulatory Commission.

full_content.analysis: The revocation of Jinzhou Bank Co., Ltd.'s public offering securities investment fund sales business license by the China Securities Regulatory Commission marks a significant regulatory action. This decision, grounded in the Administrative Licensing Law and related regulations, underscores the importance of compliance within the banking sector. The impact on Jinzhou Bank could be profound, limiting its ability to engage in investment fund activities and potentially diminishing its competitive edge in the financial market. This move reflects the regulatory body's commitment to maintaining standards in the banking industry, which may influence investor confidence and market dynamics in Liaoning province and beyond.

chinese_original:
近期，锦州银行股份有限公司向中国证监会提交了注销公募证券投资基金销售业务许可证的申请。根据《行政许可法》《证券投资基金法》《公开募集证券投资基金销售机构监督管理办法》等有关规定，中国证监会决定注销锦州银行股份有限公司公募证券投资基金销售业务许可证。

特此公告。

辽宁证监局

2026年1月9日
english_translation:
Recently, Jinzhou Bank Co., Ltd. submitted an application to the China Securities Regulatory Commission for the cancellation of its public offering securities investment fund sales business license. In accordance with the relevant provisions of the Administrative Licensing Law, the Securities Investment Fund Law, and the Measures for the Supervision and Administration of Public Offering Securities Investment Fund Sales Institutions, the China Securities Regulatory Commission has decided to revoke the public offering securities investment fund sales business license of Jinzhou Bank Co., Ltd.

This announcement is hereby made.

Liaoning Securities Regulatory Bureau

January 9, 2026

impact_analysis.details: The revocation of Jinzhou Bank's license may impact its operational capabilities in the investment fund sector, potentially affecting its market position.

title: China's Central Economic Work Conference Proposes Innovations in Financial Technology Services
description: The Central Economic Work Conference in late 2025 emphasized the need for innovation in financial technology services as part of the '14th Five-Year Plan'. This includes enhancing financial support for technological innovation and establishing financial technology reform pilot zones to optimize resource allocation.

key_points.details: The Central Economic Work Conference emphasizes the need for innovative financial technology services to enhance adaptability and effectiveness in the banking and insurance sectors.
Financial technology is crucial for supporting the lifecycle of technology-based enterprises, facilitating their growth through improved financial services and funding access.

full_content.analysis: The vigorous development of financial technology is pivotal for China's economic strategy, particularly in enhancing the synergy between finance and technological innovation. By focusing on innovative financial services, the policy aims to strengthen the banking and insurance sectors, ensuring they can effectively support technology-based enterprises. This approach not only addresses the financing needs of these enterprises but also promotes structural reforms within the financial system. The establishment of financial technology reform pilot zones further exemplifies the commitment to creating a robust ecosystem that facilitates the integration of technology and finance, ultimately driving sustainable economic growth and technological self-reliance.

chinese_original:
科技金融是推动科技与金融双向促进的重要支撑。2025年底召开的中央经济工作会议提出，创新科技金融服务。“十五五”规划建议对“加快建设金融强国”作出部署，强调大力发展科技金融、绿色金融、普惠金融、养老金融、数字金融。金融监管总局等部门联合发布《银行业保险业科技金融高质量发展实施方案》，旨在切实提升科技金融服务的适配性、针对性和有效性。本期特邀专家围绕相关问题进行研讨。

推动科技与金融双向促进的重要支撑

大力发展科技金融的重要意义何在？我国创新科技金融服务取得哪些成果？

王刚（国务院发展研究中心金融研究所宏观金融研究室主任、研究员）：科技金融被列为金融“五篇大文章”之首，是实现高水平科技自立自强和建设科技强国的坚实支撑。

科技金融在我国的发展可追溯到1985年中共中央作出的《关于科学技术体制改革的决定》，鼓励部门、企业和社会集团向科学技术投资，强调银行要积极开展科学技术信贷业务。科技金融并非“科技+金融”的简单叠加，而是以科技创新活动为服务对象，通过金融制度、产品工具、服务模式和生态体系多维度创新，培育金融资源与科技要素高效对接的综合性金融服务体系。从服务内容来看，科技金融涵盖信贷、股权、债券、保险、担保、租赁等多元金融工具，涉及银行、证券、保险、基金、政府部门、科研院所等多方主体。从基本特征来看，科技金融突破了传统“看报表、重抵押、求短期”的风控逻辑，转向“看技术、重专利、谋长远”的价值判断。

“十五五”时期是基本实现社会主义现代化夯实基础、全面发力的关键时期，战略机遇和风险挑战并存，大力发展科技金融具有重要意义。其一，有助于加快推进高水平科技自立自强和科技强国建设。我国要在关键核心技术领域突破“卡脖子”难题，需构建多层次科技金融服务体系。其二，有助于深化金融供给侧结构性改革。科技金融通过支持研发成果的产业化应用和市场化推广，为传统产业改造升级提供金融支持。其三，有助于拓宽金融服务边界。在净息差收窄的背景下，科技金融的发展为金融机构开拓出投贷联动等新增长点。

近年来，我国深入实施创新驱动发展战略，做好科技金融大文章，强化科技型企业全生命周期金融服务，推动更多金融资源服务于科技创新领域，促进科技创新和产业创新深度融合。

政策引领与体系构建成效显著。《中共中央关于进一步全面深化改革、推进中国式现代化的决定》提出，构建同科技创新相适应的科技金融体制，加强对国家重大科技任务和科技型中小企业的金融支持。2025年，金融监管总局等部门联合发布的《银行业保险业科技金融高质量发展实施方案》提出，未来5年，银行业保险业加快构建同科技创新相适应的金融服务体制机制。科技部、中国人民银行等部门联合印发《加快构建科技金融体制 有力支撑高水平科技自立自强的若干政策举措》，旨在加快建设具有中国特色的科技金融体系，形成多元化、多层次、多渠道的科技投入格局。

科技金融规模持续扩大，资金供给能力显著增强。中国人民银行数据显示，截至2025年三季度末，我国高新技术企业贷款余额18.84万亿元，科技型中小企业贷款余额3.56万亿元，增速均高于各项贷款平均水平；获得贷款支持的高新技术企业和科技型中小企业分别为26.66万家、27.54万家，企业获贷率均突破50%，金融服务覆盖面持续扩大。多层次资本市场支持力度持续加大，为科创企业提供了长期稳定的资金来源。截至2026年1月6日，科创板上市公司数量600家，总市值超10万亿元，总融资额超1.1万亿元。

服务实体经济质效提升，重点领域突破明显。中国人民银行通过设立科技创新再贷款和设备更新改造专项再贷款等工具，对国家重大科技项目，处于初创期、成长期的科技型中小企业，以及重点领域数字化、高端化、智能化、绿色化技术改造和设备更新提供支持。“十四五”期间，我国科研技术贷款、制造业中长期贷款、基础设施贷款年均增长27.2%、21.7%、10.1%，对先进制造业、高技术制造业、战略性新兴产业领域的金融支持不断强化。

2025年底召开的中央经济工作会议部署今年经济工作要抓好的重点任务时明确提出，创新科技金融服务。党的二十届四中全会对“加快高水平科技自立自强，引领发展新质生产力”作出部署。“十五五”时期，应抓住新一轮科技革命和产业变革历史机遇，全面增强自主创新能力，抢占科技发展制高点。

发展科技金融是促进科技创新和产业创新深度融合的必由之路，金融资本是支撑高水平科技自立自强的重要力量。当前和未来一个时期，应从以下方面推动科技金融高质量发展。一是着力提高前瞻性和包容性。新质生产力往往源自技术性突破，需要能够承担长期风险、容忍阶段性亏损的耐心资本深度介入，加快基础研究战略性、前瞻性、体系化布局。二是着力提高精准性和适配性。新质生产力发展覆盖从“种子期”到“成熟期”全生命周期的科创企业，其风险特征、融资需求差异较大，需摒弃“一刀切”的信贷模式，大力发展差异化、精细化的金融产品。三是着力提高协同性和多元性。单一金融机构或金融工具难以有效满足科技创新和产业创新深度融合的需求，需构建包括银行信贷、资本市场、保险担保等在内的多元化、接力式金融服务体系，形成支持创新的强大合力。同时，还要进一步完善科技创新风险分散分担机制。科创企业普遍具有抵质押物不足以及融资难、融资贵等问题，需进一步发挥国家融资担保基金的引领作用，加大对科技创新类中小企业风险分担和补偿力度，撬动更多金融资源向发展新质生产力聚集。

加大耐心资本投入科创领域力度

为什么科技创新更需耐心资本支持？如何鼓励更多耐心资本投入科创领域？

李凤羽（东北财经大学金融科技学院副院长、教授）：《中共中央关于进一步全面深化改革、推进中国式现代化的决定》提出，鼓励和规范发展天使投资、风险投资、私募股权投资，更好发挥政府投资基金作用，发展耐心资本。培育壮大耐心资本、引导更多耐心资本进入科技创新领域，是做好科技金融这篇大文章的应有之义。

耐心资本是指对资本回报有较长期限展望且对风险有较高承受力的资本，不以追求短期收益为首要目标，而更重视长期回报的项目或投资活动，通常不受市场短期波动干扰。从全球实践看，耐心资本主要来源于政府投资基金、养老基金、保险资本等，是私募创投基金、公募基金等引入中长期资金的重要来源，能够为投资项目、资本市场提供长期稳定的资金支持，是科技创新和产业创新的关键要素保障，是发展新质生产力的重要条件和推动力。与传统资本相比，耐心资本具有长期主义导向、价值深耕理念、风险耐受特质，致力于获取持久稳健的回报。

科技创新更需要耐心资本的支持。科技创新是发展新质生产力的核心要素，原创性、颠覆性的科技创新活动同时也具有创新成果的不确定性，不易产生立竿见影的投资回报，多数尚在初创期的科技企业通常会面临融资难、融资贵等问题。耐心资本不只关注企业的短期收益状况，更看重企业的技术创新能力、领先优势、商业模式等非财务指标和发展潜力，能够为投资项目提供长期稳定的资金支持，陪伴企业成长。因此，加快培育和发展新质生产力，耐心资本将起到重要作用。

发展耐心资本是引导生产要素向新质生产力集聚的重要动力。新质生产力依托技术革命性突破与产业深度转型，具备高科技、高效能、高质量特征，但其发展面临长周期、高风险、高投入的挑战，与传统资本的短期回报逻辑存在一定矛盾。耐心资本通过优化配置、聚焦长远，推动社会资本从短期逐利转向长期价值投资，从而引导金融资源持续流向硬科技攻关与关键核心技术研发等重点领域。

作为实现高水平科技自立自强的关键支撑，发展耐心资本可从以下方面发挥重要作用。一是匹配超长创新周期，为技术从研发到产业化提供持续、稳定的资金保障。二是管理前沿技术的不确定性，通过构建投资组合与长期持有，系统性分散和承担高风险。三是赋能创新生态，整合技术、人才与供应链资源，加速技术迭代与商业化应用进程。四是弥补市场失灵，发挥政策性金融的战略引导与托底作用。

“十四五”期间，我国积极培育壮大耐心资本、支持科技创新，尤其在硬科技攻关、科技成果转化及产业链自主可控方面成效显著。国家集成电路产业投资基金二期启动以来，重点投向芯片制造、设备、材料等薄弱环节，推动国产芯片自给率大幅攀升。科创板上市企业多集中于集成电路、生物医药、新能源等战略性新兴产业，形成了全链条产业集群，凸显硬科技属性。引导私募股权创投基金投入科创领域，如深圳市政府引导基金，自2015年起累计撬动近5000亿元资本，支持8000多个产业项目，培育专精特新企业超1500家、上市公司近600家，形成覆盖全生命周期的投资体系。自2025年5月债券市场“科技板”推出后，新政口径下的科创债已发行1681只，总规模近1.87万亿元。

耐心资本在支持科技创新中展现出巨大潜力，同时也面临结构性短板。一是供给结构不平衡。资本来源过度依赖政府基金，市场化资本入市不足。基金存续周期偏短，与硬科技研发所需的超长周期不匹配。二是市场生态不健全。退出渠道单一，专业中介服务稀缺，抑制了对早期高风险项目的投资意愿。三是激励考核机制错位。对投早、投小、投长期、投硬科技的激励不足。四是跨周期能力薄弱。社会资本风险偏好收缩，导致其本应发挥的逆周期稳定器功能难以有效实现。未来，引导更多长期资本投入科技创新，可从以下方面着力。

拓宽源头活水。提高养老金、保险资金等对早期硬科技基金的投资比例，并给予税收激励。设立超长期限的国家战略科技耐心资本母基金，专注战略目标而非短期财务回报。

优化市场生态。深化科创板改革，提升对未盈利硬科技企业的包容性。大力培育私募股权二级市场，为资本提供流动性出口。培育复合型投资人才和技术尽职调查机构，降低投资风险。

完善激励与考核机制。对政府及国有创投机构推行长周期考核，重点评估其技术孵化与产业链培育的战略价值，建立尽职免责清单。实施更大力度的税收优惠，如对投资早期科技企业的基金给予更高额度的税前加计扣除等。

强化跨周期能力。设立逆周期调节基金，授权其在市场低迷期加大对核心科技领域的定向投资。加强跨部门政策协同与预期管理，引导资本进行逆周期布局。

拓展科创金融改革试验区建设成果

近年来，我国先后成立了多个科创金融改革试验区，目前创新进展如何？

李俊成（中国社会科学院金融研究所副研究员、国家金融与发展实验室科技金融与发展研究中心副主任）：近年来，我国加大对科技创新的金融支持力度，先后成立了多个科创金融改革试验区。这是为深化金融供给侧结构性改革、强化对科技创新金融支持而批准设立的重要试点，其核心在于通过体制机制创新，打通科技、产业与金融之间的循环堵点，着力降低科创企业融资成本，优化金融资源配置。

2021年，中国人民银行等部门联合印发《山东省济南市建设科创金融改革试验区总体方案》，济南市获批全国首个科创金融改革试验区，力争通过5年左右时间，基本形成体系健全、结构合理、配套完善、保障有力的科创金融服务体系，建立完善覆盖科技创新全周期的金融生态链。2022年，《上海市、南京市、杭州市、合肥市、嘉兴市建设科创金融改革试验区总体方案》印发，目标是通过5年左右时间，将上海市、南京市、杭州市、合肥市、嘉兴市科创金融改革试验区打造成为科创金融合作示范区、产品业务创新集聚区、改革政策先行先试区、金融生态建设样板区、产城深度融合领先区。2023年，《北京市中关村国家自主创新示范区建设科创金融改革试验区总体方案》印发，力争通过5年时间，构建金融有效支持科技创新的体制机制，逐步形成与国际标准相衔接的金融制度规则，建设具有全球影响力的科创金融服务体系。

目前，已初步形成多点布局、梯次推进的试验格局。各试验区围绕科创企业全生命周期需求，在制度设计、发展路径、信用评价、激励约束等方面深入探索，形成一批具有示范价值的经验做法。

以制度创新为引领，系统构建科创金融政策框架。各试验区以标准建设和基础性制度为先导，为科创企业成长构筑了坚实的制度保障。以嘉兴市为例，作为国家科创金融改革试验区中唯一的非直辖市、非省会城市，颁布了全国首部科创金融专项法规《嘉兴市科创金融促进条例》，并制定了首个科技支行地方标准，不仅为当地科创企业营造了稳定、可预期的金融环境，也为全国科创金融制度创新贡献了“嘉兴样本”。

坚持因地制宜，探索特色发展路径。各试验区立足自身产业与金融资源，积极构建各具特色的发展模式。合肥市作为中部地区省会城市，集聚有限资源，前瞻布局战略性新兴产业，充分发挥国有资本的催化作用，走出一条集群发展的特色路径。截至“十四五”末，累计滚动注入国有资本金超2200亿元，带动投资超8400亿元，推动落地13个百亿级投资项目。通过国资引领、以投带引，包括新型显示、集成电路、新能源汽车等在内的产业集群快速崛起，不仅壮大了资本市场的“合肥板块”，也带动了区域经济能级的整体提升。

强化数智化支撑，创新信用评价模式。针对科创企业普遍缺乏抵押物导致的融资难题，南京市推出知识产权金融专业服务平台“我的麦田”。该平台基于大数据建模，以涵盖多维度创新能力的综合评价替代传统的单一知识产权估值，明显提高了授信评估的准确性。同样，杭州市建成“杭创E站”科创企业服务平台，通过脱敏处理企业授权的政务数据，从技术实力、研发投入、知识产权及市场潜力等多方面分析研判，有效推动企业数据价值向融资资本转化。各试验区依托数智化工具，有效破解了科创企业融资中的信用评估难题。

健全激励约束与容错机制，提高资本风险承担意愿。各试验区着力构建导向明确、包容审慎的激励约束与尽职免责体系，有效引导金融与社会资本投向科技创新。南京、济南、北京等地创新金融机构考核机制，通过争取内部定价优惠、制定差异化绩效方案、延长信贷人员考核周期并提高不良容忍度等方式，提升金融机构介入科创领域的积极性。杭州市将研究开发经费视同企业业绩利润，激励国企加大关键核心技术研发投入。上海、合肥、南京、嘉兴等地严格落实“三个区分开来”，细化容错认定标准与程序，积极营造鼓励创新、宽容失误的制度环境。

同时也要看到，科创金融改革试验区建设仍存在短板弱项，如政策支持与市场机制的协同效应有待加强、信息孤岛现象依然存在、知识产权等无形资产的价值评估与流转体系尚不健全等。未来，应进一步完善“政策引导+市场驱动”激励相容机制，推动数据联通与共享，健全知识产权评估与交易体系，将试点创新成果转化为制度性安排，不断提升金融支持科技创新的覆盖面和可持续性，推动更多金融资源用于科技创新领域，加快实现高水平科技自立自强和科技强国建设目标。
english_translation:
Financial technology is an important support for promoting the mutual advancement of science and technology and finance. The Central Economic Work Conference held at the end of 2025 proposed to innovate financial technology services. The "14th Five-Year Plan" suggests deploying efforts to "accelerate the construction of a strong financial nation," emphasizing the vigorous development of financial technology, green finance, inclusive finance, pension finance, and digital finance. The Financial Regulatory Administration and other departments jointly issued the "Implementation Plan for High-Quality Development of Financial Technology in the Banking and Insurance Industries," aimed at effectively enhancing the adaptability, targeting, and effectiveness of financial technology services. This issue features invited experts discussing related topics.

What is the significance of vigorously developing financial technology? What achievements have been made in our country's innovative financial technology services?

Wang Gang (Director of the Macroeconomic Finance Research Office, Financial Research Institute, Development Research Center of the State Council): Financial technology is listed as the foremost of the "five major articles" in finance and serves as a solid support for achieving high-level technological self-reliance and building a strong technological nation.

The development of financial technology in our country can be traced back to the "Decision on the Reform of the Scientific and Technological System" made by the Central Committee of the Communist Party of China in 1985, which encouraged departments, enterprises, and social groups to invest in science and technology and emphasized that banks should actively engage in scientific and technological credit business. Financial technology is not merely a simple combination of "technology + finance," but rather a comprehensive financial service system that innovates across multiple dimensions—financial systems, products, tools, service models, and ecological systems—targeting technological innovation activities and fostering efficient connections between financial resources and technological elements. In terms of service content, financial technology encompasses diverse financial tools such as credit, equity, bonds, insurance, guarantees, and leasing, involving multiple entities including banks, securities, insurance, funds, government departments, and research institutions. From a fundamental characteristic perspective, financial technology breaks through the traditional risk control logic of "looking at balance sheets, emphasizing collateral, and seeking short-term gains," shifting towards a value judgment of "focusing on technology, emphasizing patents, and planning for the long term."

The "14th Five-Year" period is a critical time for laying a solid foundation for the basic realization of socialist modernization and making comprehensive efforts, where strategic opportunities coexist with risks and challenges. Vigorously developing financial technology is of great significance. Firstly, it helps accelerate the advancement of high-level technological self-reliance and the construction of a strong technological nation. To break through the "bottleneck" problems in key core technology fields, our country needs to build a multi-level financial technology service system. Secondly, it helps deepen the structural reform of the financial supply side. Financial technology supports the industrial application and market promotion of research and development results, providing financial support for the transformation and upgrading of traditional industries. Thirdly, it helps broaden the boundaries of financial services. Against the backdrop of narrowing net interest margins, the development of financial technology opens up new growth points for financial institutions, such as investment-loan linkage.

In recent years, our country has deeply implemented the innovation-driven development strategy, focusing on the significant role of financial technology, strengthening financial services for the entire lifecycle of technology-based enterprises, and promoting more financial resources to serve the field of technological innovation, facilitating the deep integration of technological and industrial innovation.

The effectiveness of policy guidance and system construction is significant. The "Decision of the Central Committee of the Communist Party of China on Further Deepening Reform and Promoting Chinese-style Modernization" proposes to build a financial technology system that aligns with technological innovation and strengthen financial support for major national technological tasks and technology-based small and medium-sized enterprises. In 2025, the "Implementation Plan for High-Quality Development of Financial Technology in the Banking and Insurance Industries," jointly issued by the Financial Regulatory Administration and other departments, proposed that in the next five years, the banking and insurance industries should accelerate the construction of a financial service system that aligns with technological innovation. The Ministry of Science and Technology, the People's Bank of China, and other departments jointly issued "Policies and Measures to Accelerate the Construction of a Financial Technology System to Strongly Support High-Level Technological Self-Reliance," aiming to accelerate the establishment of a financial technology system with Chinese characteristics and form a diversified, multi-level, and multi-channel pattern of technological investment.

The scale of financial technology continues to expand, and the capacity for funding supply has significantly increased. Data from the People's Bank of China shows that by the end of the third quarter of 2025, the loan balance for high-tech enterprises in our country reached 18.84 trillion yuan, and the loan balance for technology-based small and medium-sized enterprises was 3.56 trillion yuan, with growth rates exceeding the average level of various loans; the number of high-tech enterprises and technology-based small and medium-sized enterprises receiving loan support reached 266,600 and 275,400, respectively, with loan acquisition rates exceeding 50%, and the coverage of financial services continues to expand. The support from multi-level capital markets has increased, providing long-term stable funding sources for technology innovation enterprises. As of January 6, 2026, the number of companies listed on the Sci-Tech Innovation Board reached 600, with a total market value exceeding 10 trillion yuan and total financing exceeding 1.1 trillion yuan.

The quality and effectiveness of services for the real economy have improved, with significant breakthroughs in key areas. The People's Bank of China has supported major national technological projects, technology-based small and medium-sized enterprises in their startup and growth phases, as well as digitalization, high-end, intelligent, and green technological transformation and equipment updates in key areas through tools such as special re-loans for technological innovation and equipment renewal and transformation. During the "14th Five-Year" period, the annual growth rates of loans for scientific research and technology, medium- and long-term loans for manufacturing, and infrastructure loans were 27.2%, 21.7%, and 10.1%, respectively, with continuous strengthening of financial support for advanced manufacturing, high-tech manufacturing, and strategic emerging industries.

The Central Economic Work Conference held at the end of 2025 clearly proposed that this year's economic work should focus on innovating financial technology services. The Fourth Plenary Session of the 20th Central Committee of the Communist Party of China deployed efforts to "accelerate high-level technological self-reliance and lead the development of new quality productivity." During the "14th Five-Year" period, we should seize the historical opportunities presented by the new round of technological revolution and industrial transformation, comprehensively enhance our independent innovation capabilities, and seize the commanding heights of technological development.

Developing financial technology is an essential path for promoting the deep integration of technological and industrial innovation, and financial capital is an important force supporting high-level technological self-reliance. In the current and future periods, we should promote the high-quality development of financial technology from the following aspects. Firstly, focus on improving foresight and inclusiveness. New quality productivity often stems from technological breakthroughs, requiring patient capital that can bear long-term risks and tolerate temporary losses to deeply engage and accelerate the strategic, forward-looking, and systematic layout of basic research. Secondly, focus on improving precision and adaptability. The development of new quality productivity covers the entire lifecycle of technology innovation enterprises from "seed stage" to "mature stage," with significant differences in risk characteristics and financing needs, necessitating the abandonment of a "one-size-fits-all" credit model and the vigorous development of differentiated and refined financial products. Thirdly, focus on improving synergy and diversity. A single financial institution or financial tool is insufficient to effectively meet the needs of deep integration between technological and industrial innovation; a diversified, relay-style financial service system that includes bank credit, capital markets, insurance guarantees, etc., needs to be constructed to form a strong synergy supporting innovation. At the same time, it is necessary to further improve the risk dispersion and sharing mechanism for technological innovation. Technology innovation enterprises generally face issues such as insufficient collateral and difficulties in financing, which necessitates further leveraging the leading role of the National Financing Guarantee Fund to increase risk sharing and compensation for technology innovation small and medium-sized enterprises, thereby attracting more financial resources to gather towards the development of new quality productivity.

Increase the investment of patient capital in the technology innovation field.

Why does technological innovation require more support from patient capital? How to encourage more patient capital to invest in the technology innovation field?

Li Fengyu (Vice Dean and Professor at the School of Financial Technology, Northeast University of Finance and Economics): The "Decision of the Central Committee of the Communist Party of China on Further Deepening Reform and Promoting Chinese-style Modernization" proposes to encourage and regulate the development of angel investment, venture capital, and private equity investment, better leveraging the role of government investment funds, and developing patient capital. Cultivating and expanding patient capital and guiding more patient capital into the technology innovation field is essential for effectively addressing the significant role of financial technology.

Patient capital refers to capital that has a long-term outlook for returns and a high tolerance for risk, not primarily pursuing short-term gains but focusing more on long-term returns from projects or investment activities, typically unaffected by short-term market fluctuations. From global practice, patient capital mainly comes from government investment funds, pension funds, and insurance capital, serving as an important source of medium- and long-term funds for private equity and venture capital funds, providing long-term stable funding support for investment projects and capital markets, and is a key element in ensuring technological and industrial innovation, as well as a vital condition and driving force for developing new quality productivity. Compared to traditional capital, patient capital has a long-term orientation, a value-deepening philosophy, and risk tolerance characteristics, aiming to achieve sustainable and stable returns.

Technological innovation requires the support of patient capital. Technological innovation is a core element in developing new quality productivity, and original and disruptive technological innovation activities also carry uncertainties regarding innovation outcomes, making it difficult to generate immediate investment returns. Most technology enterprises in their early stages typically face challenges in financing and high financing costs. Patient capital not only focuses on the short-term profit status of enterprises but also values non-financial indicators and development potential such as technological innovation capabilities, competitive advantages, and business models, providing long-term stable funding support and accompanying enterprise growth. Therefore, in accelerating the cultivation and development of new quality productivity, patient capital will play an important role.

Developing patient capital is an important driving force for guiding production factors to concentrate on new quality productivity. New quality productivity relies on revolutionary breakthroughs in technology and deep industrial transformation, characterized by high technology, high efficiency, and high quality. However, its development faces challenges of long cycles, high risks, and high investments, which contradict the short-term return logic of traditional capital. Patient capital promotes the shift of social capital from short-term profit-seeking to long-term value investment through optimized allocation and a focus on the long term, thereby guiding financial resources to continuously flow towards hard technology breakthroughs and key core technology research and development.

As a key support for achieving high-level technological self-reliance, developing patient capital can play an important role in the following aspects. Firstly, it matches the ultra-long innovation cycle, providing continuous and stable funding guarantees for the transition from research and development to industrialization. Secondly, it manages the uncertainties of cutting-edge technologies by constructing investment portfolios and holding them long-term, systematically dispersing and bearing high risks. Thirdly, it empowers the innovation ecosystem by integrating technology, talent, and supply chain resources, accelerating the process of technological iteration and commercialization. Fourthly, it compensates for market failures, playing a strategic guiding and bottom-line role for policy-based finance.

During the "14th Five-Year" period, our country has actively cultivated and expanded patient capital to support technological innovation, achieving significant results, especially in hard technology breakthroughs, technology transfer, and ensuring the autonomy of the industrial chain. Since the launch of the second phase of the National Integrated Circuit Industry Investment Fund, it has focused on weak links such as chip manufacturing, equipment, and materials, significantly increasing the self-sufficiency rate of domestic chips. Companies listed on the Sci-Tech Innovation Board are concentrated in strategic emerging industries such as integrated circuits, biomedicine, and new energy, forming a full-chain industrial cluster that highlights hard technology attributes. Guiding private equity and venture capital funds to invest in the technology innovation field, such as the Shenzhen Municipal Government Guidance Fund, has leveraged nearly 500 billion yuan in capital since 2015, supporting over 8,000 industrial projects and nurturing more than 1,500 specialized and innovative enterprises and nearly 600 listed companies, forming an investment system covering the entire lifecycle. Since the launch of the "Technology Board" in the bond market in May 2025, 1,681 technology innovation bonds have been issued under the new policy framework, with a total scale of nearly 1.87 trillion yuan.

Patient capital has shown great potential in supporting technological innovation but also faces structural shortcomings. Firstly, there is an imbalance in the supply structure. Capital sources are overly reliant on government funds, with insufficient market-oriented capital entering the market. The fund duration is relatively short, which does not match the ultra-long cycles required for hard technology research and development. Secondly, the market ecosystem is not well-developed. The exit channels are singular, and professional intermediary services are scarce, inhibiting investment willingness in early high-risk projects. Thirdly, the incentive and assessment mechanisms are misaligned. There is insufficient incentive for early, small, long-term, and hard technology investments. Fourthly, the ability to operate across cycles is weak. The risk appetite of social capital has contracted, making it difficult to effectively realize its intended counter-cyclical stabilizing function. In the future, guiding more long-term capital into technological innovation can focus on the following aspects.

Broaden the source of fresh water. Increase the investment ratio of pension funds, insurance funds, etc., in early hard technology funds and provide tax incentives. Establish a national strategic technology patient capital mother fund with an ultra-long duration, focusing on strategic goals rather than short-term financial returns.

Optimize the market ecosystem. Deepen the reform of the Sci-Tech Innovation Board, enhancing its inclusiveness towards unprofitable hard technology enterprises. Vigorously cultivate a secondary market for private equity to provide liquidity exits for capital. Foster composite investment talents and technical due diligence institutions to reduce investment risks.

Improve incentive and assessment mechanisms. Implement long-cycle assessments for government and state-owned venture capital institutions, focusing on evaluating their strategic value in technology incubation and industrial chain cultivation, and establish a list of due diligence exemptions. Implement more substantial tax incentives, such as higher pre-tax deductions for funds investing in early-stage technology enterprises.

Strengthen cross-cycle capabilities. Establish a counter-cyclical adjustment fund, authorizing it to increase targeted investments in core technology fields during market downturns. Enhance inter-departmental policy coordination and expectation management to guide capital towards counter-cyclical layouts.

Expand the achievements of the construction of financial technology reform pilot zones.

In recent years, our country has established several financial technology reform pilot zones. What is the current state of innovation progress?

Li Junchen (Associate Researcher at the Financial Research Institute of the Chinese Academy of Social Sciences, Deputy Director of the Technology Finance and Development Research Center of the National Financial and Development Laboratory): In recent years, our country has increased financial support for technological innovation and established several financial technology reform pilot zones. These are important pilot projects approved to deepen the structural reform of the financial supply side and strengthen financial support for technological innovation, focusing on innovating systems and mechanisms to eliminate the bottlenecks in the circulation between technology, industry, and finance, aiming to reduce financing costs for technology innovation enterprises and optimize the allocation of financial resources.

In 2021, the People's Bank of China and other departments jointly issued the "Overall Plan for the Construction of the Financial Technology Reform Pilot Zone in Jinan City, Shandong Province," making Jinan the first national financial technology reform pilot zone, aiming to establish a sound, reasonable, well-supported financial technology service system covering the entire cycle of technological innovation within about five years. In 2022, the "Overall Plan for the Construction of Financial Technology Reform Pilot Zones in Shanghai, Nanjing, Hangzhou, Hefei, and Jiaxing" was issued, with the goal of transforming these pilot zones into demonstration areas for financial technology cooperation, innovation clusters for product and business, pioneering areas for reform policies, model areas for financial ecosystem construction, and leading areas for deep integration of industry and city within about five years. In 2023, the "Overall Plan for the Construction of the Financial Technology Reform Pilot Zone in the Zhongguancun National Independent Innovation Demonstration Zone in Beijing" was issued, aiming to build a system and mechanism for effective financial support for technological innovation within five years, gradually forming financial institutional rules that align with international standards and establishing a globally influential financial technology service system.

Currently, a preliminary multi-point layout and step-by-step advancement of the pilot zones have formed. Each pilot zone has deeply explored aspects such as institutional design, development paths, credit evaluation, and incentive

impact_analysis.details: The development of financial technology enhances banks' ability to provide tailored financial services, improving their competitiveness and adaptability.
Innovative financial technology fosters new investment opportunities, enabling investment services to better support technology-driven enterprises and projects.
Financial technology advancements allow insurance brokers to offer more customized products, improving risk assessment and client engagement in the technology sector.
